descript.abstract: Abstract: The Massachusetts Department of Transportation Highway Division Traffic Inventory contains the spatial linework for all the public and a good portion of the private roadways in Massachusetts, along with roadway attributes covering the roadway classification, ownership, traffic volumes, and more.
descript.purpose:
Purpose: The Traffic Inventory is a GIS-based asset management system for the state's highway transportation system. As such, its strengths are in describing the configuration and condition of public roads and rights-of-way. It is not designed to support route-finding (e.g., shortest path applications), nor is it designed to support geocoding (although in theory intersection-based geocoding could be set up on it). It is part of the official documentation of the state road system and is used to prepare the yearly Highway Performance Monitoring System (HPMS) report to the Federal Highway Administration (FHWA).
***Note: In 2023 many local roads were excluded because their AADT values are modeled and not counted. Due to popular demand, we have included these local roads with modeled values for the 2024 Traffic Inventory. These modeled counts can be distinguished through the field AADT Derivation = Modeled Count (25). We advise that any comparisons between the two years take this discrepancy into account and that any users are aware that the modeled values do not reflect actual AADT counts.

timeperd.current: The geographical extent of this dataset is the state of Massachusetts and the temporal extent is the year 2023

idinfo.useconst: This data is in the public domain and therefore can be used by anyone for any purpose. MassDOT shall not be held liable for any errors in this data. This includes errors of omission, commission, errors concerning the content of the data, and relative and positional accuracy of the data. This data cannot be construed to be a legal document. Primary sources from which this data was compiled must be consulted for verification of information contained in this data.

cntorgp.cntorg: Massachusetts Department of Transportation Office of Transportation Planning GIS Services

idinfo.datacred: MassDOT Office of Transportation Planning, Central Transportation Planning Staff

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: Unique identifier for contiguous sequence of road segments belonging to a logical route

attrdomv.udom: Route system abbreviation plus up to five-digit number plus space plus direction abbreviation.

attr.attrdef: The measured length along the specified Route feature where this RoadInventory segment starts
The measured length along the Route feature where this RoadInventory segment ends

edom.edomvd: Not a numbered route (Arterial and Collector)

attr.attrdef: The official route number designation; need not be exclusively numeric (146A, for example)
The direction a route traverses. NB and EB are primary (inventory) direction routes. SB and WB are opposing (non-inventory) direction routes

County code: 1= Barnstable, 3 = Berkshire, 5 = Bristol, 7 = Dukes, 9 = Essex, 11 = Franklin, 13 = Hampden, 15 = Hampshire, 17 = Middlesex, 19 = Nantucket, 21 = Norfolk, 23 = Plymouth, 25 = Suffolk, 27 = Worcester

edom.edomvd: Nantucket Planning and Economic Development Commission
Southeastern Regional Planning and Economic Development District
Greater Attleboro Taunton Regional Transit Authority
Not covered by a regional transit authority
Principal Arterial - Other Freeways or Expressways
NHS - Major Public Transit or Multi-Modal Passenger Terminal

attr.attrdef: Number of travel lanes (for undivided roadways, number of lanes in both directions of travel, for divided roadways, number of lanes on the given segment only)
Number of travel lanes in the opposite direction of a divided roadway
Describes whether the segments length is counted towards the official statewide road centerline mileage

edom.edomvd: All vehicles over 28 feet in length excluded
Gross vehicle weight over 18 tons and greater than 2 axles excluded
Gross vehicle weight over 30 tons and greater than 4 axles excluded
Commercial vehicles over 5 tons carry capacity excluded
8AM to 930AM and 2PM to 330PM, School Days Only
Derived from counts collected on or adjacent to the section during the current year
Derived from factoring counts from the previous year count-base AADT that is less than three years old
Derived from count data that is three or more years old
Working code for principal arterial counting program
Carried forward from most recent of previous 3 years
Grown from Prior Year HPMS Network
AADT synchronized with other stations on the segment

attr.attrdef: The design hour volume as a percentage of AADT.
MS2 Code for assigning AADT values
Percent Design Hour Single-Unit Trucks and Buses

edom.edomvd: State or local government agency: Actual count
State or local government agency: Other count method
Private business or non-governmental agency: Actual count
Private business or non-governmental agency: Other count method
Data is developed or acquired using a method not identified in A, B, C, or D

attr.attrdef: Year of Single-Unit Trucks and Buses AADT
Source of Single-Unit Trucks and Buses AADT

cntorgp.cntorg: Massachusetts Department of Transportation Office of Transportation Planning GIS Services

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Content Standard for Digital Geospatial Metadata
